channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Confronting South Africa’s systemic failures: from education to economy
item.description: Nhlanhla Nxumalo reflects critically on South Africa’s persistent socio-economic challenges, highlighting the gap between academic knowledge and practical solutions. He observes that universities prod...

item.title: Recent Genetic Shifts in Humans Reveal Ongoing Evolution
item.description: A groundbreaking study published in Nature has revealed that human evolution is far from over, challenging the long-held belief that cultural advancements have largely eclipsed biological change. Rese...

item.title: UP cleric arrested in connection with fake nikah and sexual exploitation case
item.description: In a significant development from Amroha, Uttar Pradesh, a cleric named Maulana Nadeem was arrested on April 9, 2026, in connection with a complex case involving fake nikah and halala practices that l...

item.title: Gina Rinehart Ordered to Pay Royalties in Long-Running Mining Dispute While Retaining Asset Ownership
item.description: In a significant Western Australian Supreme Court ruling, mining magnate Gina Rinehart has been ordered to pay hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties to the heirs of Peter Wright, her father Lan...

item.title: Celebrity Chef Heny Sison Shares Her Journey Living with Heart Failure
item.description: Celebrity chef Heny Sison, known for her culinary expertise in the Philippines, has recently opened up about her experience living with heart failure. Despite the diagnosis, she remains active in her ...

item.title: Persistent Overcrowding at Kelowna General Hospital Raises Questions About Unused Space
item.description: The issue of 'hallway health care' continues to affect Kelowna General Hospital (KGH), where patients are being treated in corridors despite two unfinished floors remaining closed for over a decade. K...

item.title: Kenya Met Warns of Heavy Rains and Flood Risks in Several Counties Including Nairobi
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has announced that heavy rainfall and thunderstorms are expected across several regions in the country within the next 24 hours, starting from Wednesday night...

item.title: Boston Dynamics Integrates DeepMind AI Into Spot for Smarter Industrial Inspections
item.description: Boston Dynamics has upgraded its quadruped robot Spot with a new layer of intelligence by integrating Google DeepMind’s Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 system. This collaboration enhances Spot’s ability to ope...

item.title: Leak of 'Avatar: Aang, The Last Airbender' Sparks Debate Over Studio Decisions and Fan Reactions
item.description: The highly anticipated animated film 'Avatar: Aang, The Last Airbender' was leaked online six months before its scheduled Paramount+ release, creating division among fans and frustration among the ani...

item.title: US Senate Republicans defeat resolution to limit Trump's military action against Iran
item.description: The United States Senate, led by Republicans, has blocked the latest attempt by Democrats to rein in President Donald Trump’s war powers concerning Iran. Lawmakers voted down a bipartisan war‑powers r...

item.title: How changing my surname as a teenager helped me reclaim my identity
item.description: Jess O'Halloran shares how changing her surname at 14, following her parents' divorce, significantly impacted her sense of identity and wellbeing. Initially, her surname, inherited from her father, be...

item.title: Concerns over Parliamentary Expansion and Its Impact on Indian Democracy
item.description: The recent decision by the BJP-led government to convene a special parliamentary session to discuss expanding the size of the Lok Sabha has sparked significant debate. While the move is justified as n...

item.title: Kenyan courts push dialogue over court battles as mediation eases backlog
item.description: The article argues that Kenyan courts are moving away from long, costly court battles and instead encouraging dialogue through mediation to solve disputes. Mediation is portrayed as faster, cheaper an...

item.title: Treating Drug Addiction in South Africa as a Health Concern
item.description: South Africa faces a growing drug crisis, particularly in areas like Westbury, where illicit substances are prevalent. Despite the 2019-2024 National Drug Master Plan recognising addiction as a chroni...

item.title: Karachi Mayor Plans Action Against Unauthorized Banners Affecting City Cleanliness
item.description: KARACHI: The dispute between the Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) and Jamaat-i-Islami (JI) over Karachi's civic management escalated as Mayor Murtaza Wahab issued a warning to printing presses involved in...

item.title: How FRSC Reforms Are Tackling Deadly Tanker Explosions on Nigerian Roads
item.description: The article for this URL talks about the reforms the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) is implementing to reduce gruesome tanker accidents and explosions on Nigerian roads. The FRSC is tightening enfor...

item.title: Accountability Challenges in Addressing Drug Houses in Newfoundland Neighbourhoods
item.description: Residents in Newfoundland and Labrador are raising concerns about the impact of so-called 'trap houses'—residences used for drug activity—on their communities. Emails obtained by CBC News show neighbo...

item.title: Rising fuel costs squeeze Maritimes lobster harvesters despite improved prices and renewed Chinese demand
item.description: Lobster fishers in Nova Scotia and across the Maritimes are bracing for a costly season despite hopes for strong market prices driven by lower supply and renewed demand from China. The Maritime Fisher...

item.title: Defence Minister blames rise in insecurity during political periods on those seeking to undermine government
item.description: Nigeria’s Minister of Defence, retired General Christopher Gwabin Musa, don talk say the increase in insecurity wey dey happen for political periods no be ordinary matter. According to am, some people...

item.title: Anti-Immigrant Group's Street Cleaning Effort in Durban Sparks Legal and Safety Concerns
item.description: In Durban, an anti-immigrant organisation called March and March launched a week-long 'clean-up campaign' on 14 April 2026, ostensibly to sweep streets and remove rubbish. However, the campaign quickl...
